item.title: Hello, World!
item.description: This is the first post of my new blog. It's mostly for testing and style tweaking, so it's fairly sparse.

item.title: Introducing: chalk-blog
item.description: For the past few months I've started having ideas for blog articles I wanted to write. Not having an actual blog made that somewhat difficult, so naturally, that led into how I could develop my own blogging system...

item.title: Making Node CLI Apps: The Basics
item.description: Node is actually a pretty good choice when you want to make a command line application. On my journey to create chalk-blog, I had to research some of these basics and figured I would consolidate them into a single blog for posterity.

item.title: Unraveling Promises
item.description: A common issue I've seen some developers struggle with is correctly utilizing the Promise chain. Promises give us the ability to reduce callback hell in our code, but without proper understanding, they could create additional complexity instead. This easily leads into spaghetti code that is hard to follow and hard to maintain. Below, I will explain the nuances of the Promise chain using examples of complex code and how we can simplify.

item.title: ezcli
item.description: As mentioned in Introducing: chalk-blog, while working on my blogging system I pulled out various useful pieces into their own packages. These were parts of the system I thought could be useful outside the scope of chalk-blog and thus deserved their own dedicated distributions. ezcli was the first of those packages.

item.title: cmd-executor
item.description: Yet another chalk-blog spin-off, this time I want to talk about cmd-executor, which may be my favorite package I've written so far.

item.title: async/await
item.description: Ever since node 7.6.0 dropped, I have been all over the async/await bandwagon. Pretty much all of my side projects use them now instead of raw Promises (at least, as much as I can), and I'm loving the clarity they provide to my code.

item.title: Couch Views and Today
item.description: I ran into an interesting issue this past week regarding a Couch View and the concept of Today. Here's a synopsis of the problem as well as how it was fixed...

item.title: get-function-params
item.description: Continuing my (pseudo) series on chalk-blog related projects, here's a quick overview of get-function-params.

item.title: 1GAM January: Battle Pong
item.description: So I've always wanted to make a game. That's how I started getting into programming in the first place. I would make little games on my TI-83+ calculator when I was supposed to be learning Geography or something like that. However, I tend to overcomplicate things and then get bored when I don't make much progress. So this year, I decided to unofficially participate in One Game a Month (1GAM).

item.title: 1GAM February: Obligatory Zombie Game
item.description: Oh no, it's that darned zombie apocalypse... again. Good thing I have my trusty sidearm on me -- this thing packs quite a punch! Unfortunately, the trigger is jammed so I can't stop shooting. Perhaps some of these zambos will have some parts on them so I can at least increase the fire rate. Here's to the end of the world.

item.title: 1GAM March: Punching Simulator 3000
item.description: So this month I spent way too much time playing around with engine structures and basic prototypes. So I don't have a full game, but instead here is one of the little demos I was playing around with trying to make something fun.
